items.item: The /start path still fails when compact handoff or startup brief imports model-router because scripts/lib/turn-classifier.mjs is missing. Restore the pure classifier dependency so startup rendering and handoff compaction stop breaking.
items.recipe: Add scripts/lib/turn-classifier.mjs from the Studio Ops canonical pure classifier, then verify node scripts/compact-handoff.mjs and node scripts/render-startup-brief.mjs no longer fail at import time.
items.why: VaultFront's product surface is strong, but session protocol fragility wastes every future agent pass. This also restores the token-cost routing layer that keeps trivial model-router calls out of expensive model paths.

items.item: The mobile tutorial strip exists, but the remaining task board gate is still manual visual smoke. Add an automated mobile-width component smoke that proves the strip renders, advances, and records telemetry without blocking the viewport.
items.recipe: Create a focused Vitest/jsdom test for VaultFrontTutorial that stubs matchMedia to mobile, uses fake timers for the delayed open, asserts .strip content/controls, advances a step, dismisses, and verifies tutorial pulse events.
items.why: This converts the last mobile onboarding confidence gap into a repeatable gate. It protects the exact first-run path that was previously suppressed on compact screens.

items.item: VaultFront already tracks rivalry revenge progress, but the player only sees it as a contract counter. Turn that hidden retention signal into a post-match rival challenge prompt that names the next objective and pushes a rematch/requeue decision.
items.recipe: Add a WinModal rival challenge card when rivalryRevengeDelta is positive: show the revenge count, a concrete next objective, and reuse the existing rematch/requeue actions. Add a focused unit test that localStorage rivalry progress renders the prompt.
items.why: This uses an existing low-cost signal to create a sticky 'one more match' loop without any new paid AI calls. It makes player-to-player grudges legible, which is a high-retention game pattern that current UI underplays.

items.item: After shipping this pass, PROJECT_STATUS, TASK_BOARD, CURRENT_STATE, and SELF_IMPROVEMENT_LOOP need to reflect S66 evidence rather than leaving the next /start brief one session behind.
items.recipe: Update public-safe context files with the S66 shipped items, verification commands, and next milestone; validate PROJECT_STATUS silScore still equals the sum of silCategoriesV3.
items.why: VaultFront's startup brief consumes machine-readable truth first. Keeping it synchronized prevents phantom regressions in future start/closeout passes.

skipped.reason: Still requires real checkout/supporter evidence; code already prevents false clearing.
High upside but broader than this reliability+retention pass and would add paid AI pressure.
Valuable manual validation, but the current pass prioritizes automated confidence and session fragility repair.

executionLog.summary: Restored the missing model-router helper chain used by startup rendering and compact handoff: turn classifier, SIL forecaster, visual sparkline primitive, and skill-cost ledger.
Added a mobile-width component smoke for VaultFrontTutorial that proves the compact strip opens, advances, dismisses, and records tutorial pulse telemetry.
WinModal now renders a Rival Challenge card when rivalry revenge progress is earned, converting the existing counter into a visible rematch/requeue retention prompt.
Public-safe context, audit log, implementation plan, startup brief, gitignore, and PROJECT_STATUS were updated for S66 evidence with 640 passing tests and SIL 998.
